systemComplexity.label: Relationship Dimensions
systemComplexity.description: trust, fear, desire, respect, rivalry, debt

systemComplexity.label: Game Overlays
systemComplexity.description: Battle, Cards, Romance, Interrogation, Infiltration, Negotiation, Shop, Crafting, Evidence Board, Rivalry, Temptation, Duel of Wits

systemComplexity.label: Message Types
systemComplexity.description: From basic narration to intercepted radio transmissions

systemComplexity.label: Director Personas
systemComplexity.description: AI narrative personalities shaping pacing and prose

decisions.title: Depth Over Breadth
decisions.choice: Build one deeply systemic world vs. 20 shallow ones
decisions.decided: Depth first — one world with full type system, 6-axis relationships, quest objectives
decisions.tradeoff: Slower content library growth, no variety for early playtesting
decisions.lesson: One working world taught more than twenty broken ones would have

decisions.title: Multi-Model Pipeline
decisions.choice: One expensive AI call vs. three specialized calls per turn
decisions.decided: Pipeline — fast models for structure, quality models for prose
decisions.tradeoff: 3x API calls, higher latency, more complex error handling
decisions.lesson: Cost-per-turn is the wrong metric. Cost-per-wow-moment is the right one.

decisions.title: Always-Decision Default
decisions.choice: Free text input (type anything) vs. structured choices (2-4 options)
decisions.decided: Structured choices by default — AI crafts meaningful paths each turn
decisions.tradeoff: Feels less 'open' at first. Some players initially resist.
decisions.lesson: Freedom ≠ agency. Constraints create better experiences.

decisions.title: Progressive Disclosure
decisions.choice: Show everything upfront vs. reveal through gameplay
decisions.decided: Everything hidden until earned — NPCs as silhouettes, locations locked
decisions.tradeoff: Harder to market ('look at all this content!')
decisions.lesson: Discovery is the core pleasure. Listing everything kills it.

decisions.title: Provider-Agnostic AI Layer
decisions.choice: Build tightly on one AI provider vs. abstract the AI layer
decisions.decided: Abstraction from day one — unified interface, any provider implements
decisions.tradeoff: More upfront engineering. Couldn't use provider-specific features.
decisions.lesson: Swapped providers 3x in 8 days with zero game logic changes.

decisions.title: External Critique Cycle
decisions.choice: Keep building in isolation vs. actively seek harsh external audit
decisions.decided: Submitted full architecture to two independent auditors around Day 6
decisions.tradeoff: Emotionally difficult. Revealed blind spots mid-sprint.
decisions.lesson: Highest-ROI activity of the entire build. Do it earlier next time.

featuresCut.lesson: Ship single-player first. Validate the core loop.
Kill features that work 90% of the time.

featuresCut.reason: Felt random and unfair in narrative context
Players wrote trivial or system-breaking inputs

lessons.title: AI is a runtime, not a feature
lessons.summary: The interesting products aren't 'apps with AI bolted on.' They're products where AI is the execution layer.

lessons.title: Structured output > free text
lessons.summary: When the AI can return typed JSON, you can build systems on it. When it returns free text, you're parsing hope.

lessons.title: Multi-model pipelines are underrated
lessons.summary: The real question isn't 'which model is best' but 'which models, in what sequence, for what tasks.'

lessons.title: Coherence is the moat
lessons.summary: Can you stay coherent at turn 50? That requires architecture, not model quality.

lessons.title: Type systems are design tools
lessons.summary: Change a type, the compiler tells you every system that assumes the old shape.

lessons.title: Depth beats breadth at every stage
lessons.summary: One deeply systemic world taught more than twenty shallow ones would have.

lessons.title: First architecture is the most important product decision
lessons.summary: Features can be added. Architectures can't be swapped.

lessons.title: External critique is the highest-ROI activity
lessons.summary: Solo builders develop blind spots. Seek harsh feedback, respond systematically.

lessons.title: UX is half the product
lessons.summary: Same AI output, presented differently, feels completely different.

lessons.title: Kill features that work 90% of the time
